channel.title: The Hungarian Daily
channel.description: Your essential daily English-language briefing on Hungarian politics, business, and culture.
channel.itunes:author: Eric Chi

item.title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-04-14
item.description: Good morning, afternoon, or evening, depending on where youre tuning in from! And a very warm welcome to The Hungarian Daily, your essential podcast for foreign professionals, expats, digital nomads, and international business executives navigating life and work here in Budapest, Hungary.It is Monday, April 13, 2026, and what a seismic shift we have to discuss today. If youre just waking up, or perhaps still reeling from the weekends events, buckle up. Hungary has just witnessed a political earthquake of historic proportions, and the tremors are already being felt across the economy and beyond.Our top story, dominating every headline and conversation, is the **landslide victory of Péter Magyar** (Pay-ter Mah-djar) and his Tisza (Tih-sah) Party in yesterday’s parliamentary elections. This outcome has swept away the Orbán (Or-bahn) era, bringing to an end Viktor Orbáns remarkable 16-year tenure as Prime Minister. Its a moment that many thought might never come, and the capital, Budapest (Boo-da-pesht), erupted into spontaneous celebrations last night, with Reuters reporting that the city truly turned into a party zone as the news broke.

item.title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-04-15
item.description: Jó reggelt, és üdvözlöm a The Hungarian Daily mai adásában! Im your host, and its a pleasure to have you join us this Wednesday, April 15, 2026, for your essential update on everything happening in and around Budapest (Boo-da-pesht). Weve got a packed show today, delving into the latest political shifts, key economic indicators, and some exciting cultural happenings that are sure to pique your interest. Grab your kávé, lets dive in!Were going to start today with some fascinating cultural news, before moving into the seismic political shifts that have captured everyones attention, and then we’ll round out with crucial economic insights for our international professionals and business leaders.First up, for those looking to immerse themselves deeper into the rich tapestry of Hungarian culture, the Museum of Ethnography is launching a brand new special traveling exhibition called Folklore Forever – Old Patterns, New Motifs. This isnt just about looking at artifacts behind glass; its designed to be an experience to be lived and tried out. Kicking off on April 24th, this interactive exhibition promises to showcase the history and enduring legacy of Hungarian folk art, bringing it vibrantly into the present day. For expats, digital nomads, and international families, this is a fantastic opportunity to really connect with the heart of Hungarian identity. Folk motifs and melodies are not static relics; they are living traditions, constantly evolving, and this exhibition aims to demonstrate that dynamic spirit. Imagine engaging with traditional crafts, understanding the symbolism behind intricate embroidery, or perhaps even trying your hand at some folk art techniques. It’s a wonderful way to bridge cultural gaps, learn about the country you call home, and perhaps even find unique, authentic souvenirs or inspirations for your own creative endeavors. Keep an eye out for its schedule as it travels beyond Budapest, offering a glimpse into regional cultural expressions.
